scope.summary: B16a's driver prerequisite is complete: TokenSource can now carry the OCI IAM token's bound PKCS#8 proof-of-possession key without retaining a static access token.
out_of_scope: Server-side adoption: oraclemcp still pins a released driver and B16a remains open until a release carrying this driver API can be adopted.
Live OCI IAM token acceptance against an Oracle database.
Token persistence or rendering: neither is introduced; the source and key remain external, redacted inputs.

entry_points.name: ConnectOptions::with_token_source_and_key (rust-oracledb)
entry_points.trace: The checksum-bound driver commit c3885783 adds the public builder, records the source and private key while leaving access_token unset, and feeds the existing connection token/PoP path. Its six offline token_source tests pass: plaintext is refused before fetch, refresh errors are typed and redacted before dial, and each physical attempt refreshes without static-token fallback.

limitations: The source-plus-key test uses an unopened TCPS loopback endpoint. PoP signing happens after an Oracle ACCEPT packet, so this test proves source/key wiring, transport refusal, refresh, and redaction but not a live on-wire signature.
No OCI token, private key, or Oracle server was used. The test values are deliberately non-secret sentinels and all rendered errors are checked for redaction.
Cross-repo close: this checkout records a checksum-bound rust-oracledb commit; the source tree for the server itself is unchanged by this prerequisite.
